_README: Copy this file to config/system.json and fill in the values for YOUR setup. Pull the RF values (control channel, site frequency spread, system type Phase I vs II) from your RadioReference export for Orange County CCCS. Anything in ALL_CAPS or marked CHANGE_ME must be edited.

icecast._serverReachableBase: The base URL the SafeT SERVER uses to pull each stream. The bridge worker (ffmpeg) runs on the SafeT server, NOT on this PC. If you self-host SafeT on THIS same PC, leave it as 127.0.0.1. If SafeT runs in the cloud (Railway), this PC's localhost is NOT reachable from there — expose Icecast with a tunnel (see README 'Cloud SafeT') and put the public URL here, e.g. https://occcs.example.trycloudflare.com

sdr._comment: One Nooelec v5 (RTL-SDR, R820T2). ~2.4 MHz max capture; ~2.0 MHz reliable. 'gain' 0 = auto AGC; set a fixed dB (e.g. 36-49) once you tune. 'ppm' from your dongle's frequency error.

system._comment: From RadioReference. 'type' is p25 — trunk-recorder auto-detects Phase I vs Phase II (TDMA) per call, so there is NO phase flag to set. 'modulation' is the CONTROL-CHANNEL modulation and is the knob that matters: 'qpsk' for CQPSK/LSM simulcast (most P25 Phase II county systems), 'fsk4' for C4FM. If the control channel won't lock on one, try the other. List the control-channel frequency(ies) in Hz; trunk-recorder uses the first that locks.

_multiDongle: OPTIONAL — to use TWO+ dongles (a system wider than one ~2 MHz window, like the full OC CCCS spread) or add another band, replace the single 'sdr' object above with a 'sources' array, and/or the single 'system' object with a 'systems' array. The talkgroups you pick in the SafeT console always attach to the FIRST system. Example:

_systems_example._note: a second P25 system on UHF, decoded by the 2nd dongle

safet._comment: An ADMIN account on your SafeT-PTT server. baseUrl ends in /v1. agencySlug only needed for non-owner multi-tenant logins (leave "" otherwise).

bridgeDefaults._comment: Applied to every bridge unless overridden per-entry below. voxThreshold 0-1 (lower = more sensitive). yieldToUnits=false keeps scanner traffic on-air even when a real unit keys up; set true if you want live units to pre-empt the feed.

_bridges_comment: OPTIONAL — only used by the offline `npm run generate` path (B). If you pick talkgroups in the SafeT console (Bridges → Import from RadioReference) and run `npm start`, this list is IGNORED. For the offline path: 'tgid' is the decimal Talkgroup ID, 'channel' is the SafeT channel name, 'mount' should be tg<TGID> (e.g. tg16) to match the console convention.
